hexagrams.name: Qian
hexagrams.image: Heaven above, Heaven below. The Creative.
hexagrams.judgment: The Creative works sublime success, furthering through perseverance.
hexagrams.meaning: This hexagram speaks of primal power — the force of pure creative energy. It does not act through effort alone but through alignment with what is right and true. When The Creative appears, you are being called to step forward with confidence. Your ideas have force behind them. Your intentions carry weight. This is not a time for hesitation or second-guessing. The universe is aligned with your direction. Move forward with integrity and the strength of your convictions, and what you set in motion will endure.
hexagrams.guidance: Begin what you have been delaying. Your energy is at its peak.
hexagrams.warning: Do not mistake confidence for arrogance. The Creative demands humility alongside strength.

hexagrams.name: Kun
hexagrams.image: Earth above, Earth below. The Receptive.
hexagrams.judgment: The Receptive brings sublime success, furthering through the perseverance of a mare.
hexagrams.meaning: The Receptive is the complement to The Creative — not passive but profoundly nurturing. Where The Creative initiates, The Receptive completes. This hexagram calls you to listen rather than speak, to receive rather than project, to allow rather than force. There is enormous power in yielding at the right moment. The earth does not resist the rain — it absorbs it and transforms it into life. You are being asked to embody this quality. The answers you seek will not come through pushing but through stillness.
hexagrams.guidance: Be patient. What you need is coming to you. Do not reach for it.
hexagrams.warning: Receptivity without discernment becomes passivity. Know when to receive and when to act.

hexagrams.name: Zhun
hexagrams.image: Clouds and thunder. Difficulty at the Beginning.
hexagrams.judgment: Difficulty at the Beginning works supreme success, furthering through perseverance.
hexagrams.meaning: Every great beginning is accompanied by difficulty. The seed does not break through the soil easily — it pushes against resistance, and that resistance makes it strong. This hexagram appears when you are at the very start of something significant and the obstacles feel overwhelming. They are not signs of failure. They are the natural friction of creation. Do not abandon your course. Do not mistake difficulty for impossibility. The thunder rolls before the rain, and the rain is what the earth needs. Your persistence through this initial struggle will determine the strength of everything that follows.
hexagrams.guidance: Seek help from those wiser or more experienced. No one breaks new ground entirely alone.
hexagrams.warning: Do not attempt to force rapid progress. Premature action now undoes careful groundwork.

hexagrams.name: Meng
hexagrams.image: A spring wells up at the foot of the mountain. Youthful Folly.
hexagrams.judgment: Youthful Folly has success. It is not I who seek the young fool — the young fool seeks me.
hexagrams.meaning: Youthful Folly speaks of inexperience — not as a fault but as a starting point. The spring at the foot of the mountain does not know where it is going yet, but it flows. Learning requires acknowledging what you do not know. This hexagram appears as a gentle reminder that you may be approaching a situation with too much confidence in your existing understanding. True wisdom begins with recognising the limits of current knowledge. This is not a time to pretend expertise. Ask questions. Seek a teacher or mentor. The willingness to not-know is itself a form of wisdom.
hexagrams.guidance: Approach the situation as a student, not an expert. Your openness is your greatest asset now.
hexagrams.warning: Asking the same question repeatedly in hope of a different answer reveals foolishness, not persistence.

hexagrams.name: Xu
hexagrams.image: Clouds rise to heaven. Waiting.
hexagrams.judgment: Waiting. If you are sincere, you have light and success. Perseverance brings good fortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Waiting is not inaction. It is the active, conscious decision to allow the right moment to arrive rather than forcing a premature one. The clouds gathering before rain are not wasting time — they are becoming what they need to be. This hexagram calls you to trust in timing. What you want is coming. The conditions are not yet fully formed. Acting now out of impatience would be like pulling a plant from the ground to check if the roots are growing. Nourish yourself during this waiting period — rest, prepare, strengthen yourself — so that when the moment arrives you are fully ready.
hexagrams.guidance: Use this waiting period to prepare thoroughly for what you know is coming.
hexagrams.warning: Waiting with anxiety defeats its own purpose. Trust requires releasing the need to control the timeline.

hexagrams.name: Song
hexagrams.image: Heaven and water moving in opposite directions. Conflict.
hexagrams.judgment: Conflict. You are sincere and are being obstructed. A cautious halt halfway brings good fortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Conflict arises when two forces move in opposite directions. This hexagram does not condemn conflict — it counsels wisdom within it. Not every conflict should be pursued to its conclusion. Sometimes the wisest resolution is a honourable withdrawal — not defeat, but the recognition that prolonged struggle costs more than it gains. If you are in conflict now, ask honestly: Is this worth what it is taking from you? The answer may be yes. But it may also be that a graceful step back opens a better path than continued confrontation.
hexagrams.guidance: Seek mediation from a neutral third party if the conflict has become entrenched.
hexagrams.warning: Do not initiate major new projects or agreements while this conflict is unresolved.

hexagrams.name: Shi
hexagrams.image: In the middle of the earth is water. The Army.
hexagrams.judgment: The Army. The army needs perseverance and a strong man. Good fortune without blame.
hexagrams.meaning: The Army speaks of organised effort toward a collective goal — discipline, leadership, and the marshalling of resources. This hexagram appears when you are managing a complex undertaking that requires coordinating many elements or people. The strength of an army lies not in brute force but in organisation. Every element must understand its role. Every action must serve the larger purpose. This is a time to lead clearly, communicate the mission precisely, and trust those under your direction to carry their part. A strong and trustworthy leader is the essential ingredient.
hexagrams.guidance: Clarify roles and responsibilities before advancing. Confusion in command leads to defeat.
hexagrams.warning: Do not use excessive force. The army that brutalises its own people loses its mandate to lead.

hexagrams.name: Bi
hexagrams.image: On the earth is water. Holding Together.
hexagrams.judgment: Holding Together brings good fortune. Inquire of the oracle once again whether you possess sublimity, constancy, and perseverance.
hexagrams.meaning: Water on the earth seeks to unite — it flows toward other water and gathers into streams, rivers, and eventually oceans. Holding Together is the hexagram of union, alliance, and the strength found in joining with others who share your values and direction. This is not about merging with anyone available — it is about finding the right people to stand beside. Examine your current relationships and alliances. Are they built on genuine mutual benefit and shared values? If so, strengthen them. If not, this hexagram is a gentle warning that unity built on convenience alone does not hold under pressure.
hexagrams.guidance: Reach out to those you have been meaning to reconnect with. Alliance requires tending.
hexagrams.warning: Do not join groups or partnerships out of fear of being alone. Wrong alliance is worse than no alliance.

hexagrams.name: Xiao Xu
hexagrams.image: The wind drives across heaven. Small Taming.
hexagrams.judgment: Small Taming has success. Dense clouds, no rain from our western region.
hexagrams.meaning: Small Taming speaks of the power of gentle, consistent influence — the wind that shapes the landscape not through a single violent gust but through years of patient movement. The clouds gather but the rain has not yet come. The conditions are building but the breakthrough has not yet arrived. This is a time for patient, subtle effort rather than dramatic action. Small consistent steps are what will bring you to your goal. Trying to force the rain before the clouds are ready only disperses them. Trust the process you have set in motion.
hexagrams.guidance: Focus on small details and refinements now. The large breakthrough will follow naturally.
hexagrams.warning: Do not mistake the absence of dramatic progress for stagnation. Much is happening beneath the surface.

hexagrams.name: Lü
hexagrams.image: Heaven above, the lake below. Treading.
hexagrams.judgment: Treading upon the tail of the tiger. It does not bite the man. Success.
hexagrams.meaning: Treading speaks of moving carefully through a situation that contains genuine danger or requires great delicacy. The image of treading on a tiger's tail — and the tiger not biting — captures the essence: with the right conduct, what could harm you passes harmlessly. The key is impeccable behaviour. Not deception or avoidance, but genuine rightness of action. When you carry yourself with integrity, clarity, and appropriate humility, even the most dangerous situations can be navigated safely. Pay close attention to how you are carrying yourself right now.
hexagrams.guidance: Your conduct in this situation is under close observation. Let every action reflect your highest values.
hexagrams.warning: Carelessness or arrogance now invites exactly the danger you are hoping to avoid.

hexagrams.name: Tai
hexagrams.image: Heaven and earth unite. Peace.
hexagrams.judgment: Peace. The small departs, the great approaches. Good fortune. Success.
hexagrams.meaning: Peace is the hexagram of harmonious flow — when heaven and earth, yin and yang, inner and outer are in alignment. This is one of the most auspicious hexagrams in the I Ching. It speaks of a period of genuine goodness — not the absence of challenge, but the presence of rightness. Things are going well because they are aligned with what is true. Enjoy this period and use it wisely. Sow good seeds during times of peace, because peace, like all states, is impermanent. The goodness of this moment is best honoured by preparation for the cycles that follow.
hexagrams.guidance: Use this period of harmony to build, plan, and strengthen foundations for what comes next.
hexagrams.warning: Do not become complacent during peaceful periods. Neglecting the future in present comfort is the seed of future difficulty.

hexagrams.name: Pi
hexagrams.image: Heaven and earth do not unite. Standstill.
hexagrams.judgment: Standstill. Evil people do not further the perseverance of the superior man. The great departs; the small approaches.
hexagrams.meaning: Standstill is the opposite of Peace — a period when heaven and earth are separated, when communication fails, when energies move in opposition rather than harmony. This hexagram does not predict lasting failure. It identifies a period of genuine stagnation that must be endured, not forced. Trying to push forward aggressively during Standstill only exhausts you. The wise approach is to withdraw inward — strengthen your character, clarify your values, tend to what is within your control, and wait for the energies to shift. They will shift. Standstill, like all things, is temporary.
hexagrams.guidance: Retreat inward. This is a time for inner cultivation, not outer action.
hexagrams.warning: Do not pursue major new initiatives, alliances, or investments during this period.

hexagrams.name: Tong Ren
hexagrams.image: Heaven together with fire. Fellowship.
hexagrams.judgment: Fellowship with men in the open. Success. It furthers to cross the great water.
hexagrams.meaning: Fellowship is the hexagram of community, shared purpose, and the strength found in genuine human connection. It speaks specifically of fellowship in the open — transparent, inclusive, and without hidden agendas. This is a time when working with others toward a shared goal will bring success that solitary effort cannot achieve. Look around you at who shares your values and direction. These are the people to align with now. Fellowship built on common humanity and genuine respect is the foundation of everything lasting.
hexagrams.guidance: Reach across differences to find common ground. The alliance you need is closer than you think.
hexagrams.warning: Fellowship built on exclusion or factionalism ultimately fragments. Seek broad, genuine community.

hexagrams.name: Da You
hexagrams.image: Fire in heaven above. Great Possession.
hexagrams.judgment: Great Possession. Supreme success.
hexagrams.meaning: Great Possession speaks of abundance — not just material wealth but the fullness that comes from right relationship with what you have. The fire of the sun in heaven illuminates everything below without preference or exception. This hexagram appears at moments of genuine fullness — when your talents, resources, and circumstances are aligned in a way that creates real abundance. The key question it raises is not how to get more but how to manage well what you have. Great possession brings great responsibility. Generosity is the natural expression of this hexagram.
hexagrams.guidance: Share your abundance generously. What circulates freely grows; what is hoarded stagnates.
hexagrams.warning: Pride in possession is the fastest way to lose it. Hold what you have with gratitude, not ownership.

hexagrams.name: Qian
hexagrams.image: Within the earth, a mountain. Modesty.
hexagrams.judgment: Modesty creates success. The superior man carries things through.
hexagrams.meaning: Modesty is one of the most powerful hexagrams in the I Ching — and one of the most misunderstood. True modesty is not self-deprecation or false humility. It is the mountain hidden within the earth — enormous substance held quietly, without display. The person who embodies true modesty does not shrink but neither do they boast. They let their actions speak. This hexagram appears as a reminder that your greatest strength right now lies in understatement. Do not oversell yourself or your work. Let its quality become visible naturally.
hexagrams.guidance: Do not seek recognition. Do excellent work quietly and allow others to notice in their own time.
hexagrams.warning: False modesty — hiding capability out of fear of standing out — is its own form of ego. Be genuinely modest, not strategically invisible.

hexagrams.name: Yu
hexagrams.image: Thunder comes resounding out of the earth. Enthusiasm.
hexagrams.judgment: Enthusiasm. It furthers to install helpers and to set armies marching.
hexagrams.meaning: Enthusiasm is the hexagram of genuine joy and the energy it creates — not forced positivity, but the authentic delight that arises when you are aligned with what you are doing. This energy is contagious and powerful. People follow enthusiasm because it signals genuine conviction. This hexagram appears when you have found or are about to find something that truly excites you — and it calls you to act on that excitement with commitment. Mobilise the people and resources around you. Enthusiasm is one of the rare forces that multiplies when shared.
hexagrams.guidance: Share your excitement openly. Genuine enthusiasm creates followers and opens doors.
hexagrams.warning: Enthusiasm without preparation becomes recklessness. Channel this energy into organised action.

hexagrams.name: Sui
hexagrams.image: Thunder in the middle of the lake. Following.
hexagrams.judgment: Following has supreme success. Perseverance furthers. No blame.
hexagrams.meaning: Following is the art of knowing when to lead and when to yield — when to follow the current rather than fight it. True following is not weakness; it is the wisdom to recognise that sometimes the situation, the season, or the person before you knows better than your own preferences. This hexagram asks: where in your life are you resisting something that would benefit you if you simply allowed it? The lake at rest follows the contours of the earth. The thunder rolls where the air takes it. There is enormous power in adaptive alignment.
hexagrams.guidance: Identify what you have been resisting and ask honestly whether resistance is serving you.
hexagrams.warning: Following blindly without discernment is not wisdom — it is abdication. Know what you are following and why.

hexagrams.name: Gu
hexagrams.image: The wind blows low on the mountain. Work on the Decayed.
hexagrams.judgment: Work on what has been spoiled has supreme success. It furthers to cross the great water.
hexagrams.meaning: This hexagram addresses what has been neglected, corrupted, or allowed to deteriorate. The decay did not happen overnight — it accumulated gradually, through inattention or inherited problems. And it will not be repaired overnight either. This hexagram calls you to face what has gone wrong with honesty and to undertake the patient, unglamorous work of repair. This is not a glamorous task. Renovation is harder than construction. But it is deeply worthwhile work — and the I Ching promises that those who undertake it with sincerity will find supreme success.
hexagrams.guidance: Identify specifically what needs repair in your life, work, or relationships. Begin today.
hexagrams.warning: Do not blame predecessors for the decay. Understanding the cause matters, but dwelling on blame delays the repair.

hexagrams.name: Lin
hexagrams.image: The earth above the lake. Approach.
hexagrams.judgment: Approach has supreme success. Perseverance furthers. When the eighth month comes, there will be misfortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Approach speaks of a moment when something great is drawing near — an opportunity, a turning point, or a time when your influence can grow significantly. The earth approaches the lake — vast and stable meeting depth and reflection. You are in a position to make a meaningful impact right now. This hexagram is both an encouragement and a warning: the window is open, but it will not remain so indefinitely. The eighth month reference is a reminder that all auspicious periods eventually shift. Act now, with full commitment, while the approach is favourable.
hexagrams.guidance: Move toward your goal now with full commitment. The favourable moment is present but temporary.
hexagrams.warning: Do not wait for conditions to be perfect. The approach is now — perfect conditions are a myth.

hexagrams.name: Guan
hexagrams.image: The wind blows over the earth. Contemplation.
hexagrams.judgment: Contemplation. The ablution has been made, but not yet the offering. Full of trust they look up to him.
hexagrams.meaning: Contemplation is the hexagram of deep looking — not anxious analysis but pure, receptive observation. The wind moving over the earth sees everything without disturbing anything. This hexagram calls you to step back from your involvement in a situation and observe it with fresh, unattached eyes. What do you see when you remove yourself from the centre of the picture? What patterns become visible that were invisible when you were inside them? This is a time for gaining perspective — the kind that only comes from genuine stillness and honest looking.
hexagrams.guidance: Remove yourself from the situation temporarily. Observe from a position of detachment and notice what you see.
hexagrams.warning: Contemplation is not avoidance. After you have genuinely seen clearly, act on what you have observed.

hexagrams.name: Shi He
hexagrams.image: Thunder and lightning. Biting Through.
hexagrams.judgment: Biting Through has success. It is favourable to let justice be administered.
hexagrams.meaning: Biting Through addresses the obstacles that sit directly in the path of union or progress — like a bone between the teeth that prevents the mouth from closing. The image is direct: bite through. Do not avoid the obstacle, circumvent it, or negotiate endlessly with it. Remove it decisively. This hexagram often appears in situations where something must be confronted directly — a difficult conversation, a wrong that needs addressing, a barrier that has been left in place too long. The lightning and thunder together represent the swift, clear application of decisive action.
hexagrams.guidance: Address the obstacle directly and without further delay. Hesitation only strengthens it.
hexagrams.warning: Biting through requires precise force — not more than is needed, not less. Excessive force creates new problems.

hexagrams.name: Bi
hexagrams.image: Fire at the foot of the mountain. Grace.
hexagrams.judgment: Grace has success. In small matters it is favourable to undertake something.
hexagrams.meaning: Grace is the hexagram of beauty, form, and the way that elegant presentation enhances the substance beneath. The fire illuminates the mountain and reveals its beauty — it does not create the beauty, it reveals it. This hexagram reminds you that how something is expressed matters as much as what is expressed. Clarity, elegance, and care in presentation honour the substance of what you offer. At the same time, Grace cautions that form without substance is mere decoration. True grace is substance expressed beautifully.
hexagrams.guidance: Pay attention to how you present yourself, your work, and your ideas. Elegance and clarity make excellence visible.
hexagrams.warning: Do not confuse beautiful presentation with genuine substance. Ensure that what lies beneath the grace is worthy of it.

hexagrams.name: Bo
hexagrams.image: The mountain rests on the earth. Splitting Apart.
hexagrams.judgment: Splitting Apart. It does not further one to go anywhere.
hexagrams.meaning: Splitting Apart represents a period of dissolution and decline — the end of a cycle that once held together. Like a ripe fruit that falls from the tree, what is splitting apart has simply completed its time. Resistance is futile and counterproductive. The I Ching does not advise action in response to Splitting Apart but rather conscious withdrawal, conservation, and waiting. Do not attempt to hold together what is naturally separating. Do not initiate new ventures into this dissolution. Protect what is essential, let go of what is peripheral, and wait for the energy to turn.
hexagrams.guidance: Identify what truly needs preserving and focus all energy there. Let everything else go.
hexagrams.warning: This is not a time for new beginnings or expansive action. Consolidate and wait.

hexagrams.name: Fu
hexagrams.image: Thunder within the earth. Return.
hexagrams.judgment: Return. Success. Going out and coming in without error. Friends come without blame.
hexagrams.meaning: Return is the hexagram of the turning point — the moment when the darkest part of winter gives way to the first stirring of spring beneath the frozen earth. What has been lost is returning. What has been diminished is beginning to grow again. This is one of the most hopeful hexagrams in the I Ching, not because it promises immediate abundance, but because it marks the genuine turning of a cycle. The movement is small — just the first shoot of new growth — but it is real and it is the beginning of everything that follows. Do not force this growth. Welcome it.
hexagrams.guidance: Welcome the small signs of return with gratitude. The turning has begun.
hexagrams.warning: Do not exhaust the new growth by demanding too much from it too soon. Let it establish itself.

hexagrams.name: Wu Wang
hexagrams.image: Under heaven, thunder rolls. Innocence.
hexagrams.judgment: Innocence. Supreme success. Perseverance furthers. If someone is not as he should be, he has misfortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Innocence speaks of acting from a place of genuine naturalness — without hidden agenda, calculation, or artifice. It is not naivety. It is the profound simplicity of someone who acts from their deepest truth without distortion by ego or strategy. When you act from genuine innocence, you are aligned with the natural order of things, and natural order supports you. When you act from calculation or self-deception, you are out of alignment, and misfortune follows naturally. This hexagram asks: are you being completely honest with yourself about your motivations right now?
hexagrams.guidance: Simplify. Return to your most authentic motivation. Act from that place without strategy.
hexagrams.warning: Self-deception is the enemy of innocence. Look honestly at your true intentions.

hexagrams.name: Da Xu
hexagrams.image: Heaven within the mountain. Great Taming.
hexagrams.judgment: Great Taming. Perseverance furthers. Not eating at home brings good fortune. It furthers to cross the great water.
hexagrams.meaning: Great Taming is the hexagram of accumulated power held in reserve — like a volcano whose force has been building for years, or a scholar whose years of quiet study are about to find their expression. The mountain holds heaven within it. Enormous creative power has been gathered through discipline, patience, and the willingness to delay gratification. This hexagram tells you that your accumulated effort is real and significant. The time for its expression may be approaching. Continue to hold and nurture what you have built. Do not dissipate it prematurely.
hexagrams.guidance: Trust the accumulation you have built. The right moment for its full expression is approaching.
hexagrams.warning: Impatience now could dissipate what years of effort have gathered. Hold steady.

hexagrams.name: Yi
hexagrams.image: At the foot of the mountain, thunder. Nourishment.
hexagrams.judgment: Nourishment. Perseverance brings good fortune. Pay heed to the providing of nourishment and to what a man seeks to fill his own mouth with.
hexagrams.meaning: Nourishment asks a deceptively simple question: what are you feeding yourself, and what are you feeding others? This applies to food, yes, but more importantly to words, thoughts, relationships, information, and energy. What you take in shapes you. What you give out shapes those around you. This hexagram calls for a careful examination of the diet of your life — not just what enters your body but what enters your mind, what you allow into your emotional life, and what you offer to others. Nourish what is worthy of nourishment.
hexagrams.guidance: Audit your inputs. What are you consuming — mentally, emotionally, informationally — that is weakening rather than strengthening you?
hexagrams.warning: Empty nourishment — distraction, flattery, hollow entertainment — leaves you hungrier than before.

hexagrams.name: Da Guo
hexagrams.image: The lake rises above the trees. Great Preponderance.
hexagrams.judgment: Great Preponderance. The ridgepole sags to the breaking point. It furthers to have somewhere to go. Success.
hexagrams.meaning: Great Preponderance describes a situation where the weight has become too great — the ridgepole of the house is bending under a load it was not designed to carry. Something exceptional is required. Normal measures are insufficient. This hexagram appears in moments of genuine crisis or at the peak of extraordinary expansion — both situations that exceed normal capacity. The advice is not to panic but to move. There is somewhere to go. The situation demands extraordinary action, not ordinary management.
hexagrams.guidance: Take the bold action that ordinary circumstances would not require. This situation calls for something exceptional.
hexagrams.warning: Do not attempt to handle an extraordinary situation with ordinary means. The weight requires an extraordinary response.

hexagrams.name: Kan
hexagrams.image: Water flows on without accumulation. The Abysmal.
hexagrams.judgment: The Abysmal repeated. If you are sincere, you have success in your heart. Action brings high honour.
hexagrams.meaning: The Abysmal is the hexagram of water flowing through a deep gorge — surrounded by danger on both sides, with only one way through: straight ahead. Water does not hesitate. It does not try to go around the danger or turn back. It simply flows into and through the obstacle, filling every hollow, and eventually passes through. This hexagram appears at genuine moments of danger or difficulty and offers a single instruction: keep moving. Maintain your inner sincerity and truth. Do not stop. Do not turn back. Flow through.
hexagrams.guidance: Move through the danger without hesitation. Sincerity and consistency of purpose are your protection.
hexagrams.warning: Do not try to jump over or around the abyss. The only way through is through.

hexagrams.name: Li
hexagrams.image: That which is bright rises twice. The Clinging.
hexagrams.judgment: The Clinging. Perseverance furthers. It brings success. Care of the cow brings good fortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Fire clings to what it burns — it depends on fuel. This hexagram speaks of the nature of clarity and brilliance — luminous but dependent on nourishment. The sun gives light but is nourished by its own nature. The person of clarity and intelligence must similarly tend to what nourishes their clear-seeing: quiet, reflection, honest relationships, and genuine learning. This hexagram is a reminder that brilliance requires maintenance. Clarity is not a fixed possession but a quality that must be continuously tended.
hexagrams.guidance: Tend to what nourishes your clarity — rest, honesty, reflection, good input. Neglect these and the light dims.
hexagrams.warning: Brilliance without discipline burns through its fuel and goes dark. Tend the flame carefully.

hexagrams.name: Xian
hexagrams.image: A lake on top of the mountain. Influence.
hexagrams.judgment: Influence. Success. Perseverance furthers. To take a maiden to wife brings good fortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Influence is the hexagram of attraction and gentle, mutual effect — two forces drawing toward each other in a way that is genuine, unhurried, and mutually enriching. The lake on top of the mountain and the mountain beneath the lake — each affecting the other. This hexagram speaks particularly of the beginning of important relationships — romantic, professional, or spiritual. It advises openness and genuine responsiveness rather than strategy or pursuit. The influence that creates lasting connection is not engineered — it is natural, honest, and reciprocal.
hexagrams.guidance: Be genuinely open and responsive in your key relationships. Real influence comes from authenticity, not strategy.
hexagrams.warning: Manipulation and calculated influence are the opposite of what this hexagram calls for. They create superficial connection that does not last.

hexagrams.name: Heng
hexagrams.image: Thunder and wind. Duration.
hexagrams.judgment: Duration. Success. No blame. Perseverance furthers. It furthers to have somewhere to go.
hexagrams.meaning: Duration is the hexagram of constancy — the staying power that allows great things to be built and sustained over time. Thunder and wind continue without ceasing. The planets persist in their orbits. A person of duration holds their course through changing conditions — not rigidly, but with a consistent orientation toward what is true and good. This hexagram appears as both a recognition of your constancy and an encouragement to maintain it. Whatever you have been building with consistent effort over time — continue. What endures, endures because it was sustained.
hexagrams.guidance: Continue. What you have been consistently building is more significant than it may appear in any single moment.
hexagrams.warning: Persistence in the wrong direction is not a virtue. Ensure your constancy is directed toward what is genuinely worthwhile.

hexagrams.name: Dun
hexagrams.image: Mountain under heaven. Retreat.
hexagrams.judgment: Retreat. Success. In what is small, perseverance furthers.
hexagrams.meaning: Retreat is not defeat. This hexagram speaks of the strategic, dignified withdrawal that preserves strength for future action. The dark forces are advancing — not because they are more powerful, but because the time is theirs. The wise person does not waste energy fighting the tide. They retreat with grace, maintain their integrity, do not allow anger or resentment to drive their exit, and wait for the conditions to shift. This is an active and courageous choice. Knowing when to retreat requires as much wisdom as knowing when to advance.
hexagrams.guidance: Withdraw from the situation with dignity and without bitterness. Preserve your strength for better conditions.
hexagrams.warning: Do not confuse strategic retreat with giving up. Maintain your inner conviction even as you step back externally.

hexagrams.name: Da Zhuang
hexagrams.image: Thunder in heaven above. Great Power.
hexagrams.judgment: Great Power. Perseverance furthers.
hexagrams.meaning: Great Power speaks of genuine strength at its peak — a moment when ability, preparation, and timing are all aligned. This is not brute force but the kind of power that comes from rightness: doing the right thing with the right level of strength at the right moment. The danger of Great Power is the temptation to misuse it — to act from strength alone without regard for what is right. True great power restrains itself when restraint is correct. It acts decisively when action is correct. The test of genuine strength is not how much it can do but how wisely it chooses what to do.
hexagrams.guidance: You have the strength to act decisively. Use it in alignment with what is genuinely right, not merely what is expedient.
hexagrams.warning: Strength without restraint is force. Force without righteousness destroys what it seeks to build.

hexagrams.name: Jin
hexagrams.image: The sun rises over the earth. Progress.
hexagrams.judgment: Progress. The powerful prince is honoured with horses in large numbers. In a single day he is granted audience three times.
hexagrams.meaning: Progress is the hexagram of clear, visible advancement — the sun rising over the earth, illuminating everything as it goes. This is a time when your efforts and qualities are being seen and recognised. Progress comes when genuine merit meets the right conditions and the right moment. What you have been working toward is becoming visible. The sun does not force the world to see it — it rises and illuminates by its nature. Continue doing excellent work. Let the recognition come to you rather than seeking it.
hexagrams.guidance: Continue your excellent work. The recognition and advancement that are coming will find you — you do not need to pursue them.
hexagrams.warning: Seeking recognition too actively now would undermine the natural progress that is already underway.

hexagrams.name: Ming Yi
hexagrams.image: The light has sunk into the earth. Darkening of the Light.
hexagrams.judgment: Darkening of the Light. In adversity it furthers to be persevering.
hexagrams.meaning: Darkening of the Light speaks of a period when the light of clarity, recognition, or good conditions has temporarily gone underground — not extinguished, but hidden. The sun has set but it will rise again. In the meantime, your light must be protected rather than displayed. This hexagram often appears when working in difficult conditions, under oppressive circumstances, or in environments that do not recognise or value what you offer. The wisdom here is to maintain your inner brightness even while concealing it from those who would harm it.
hexagrams.guidance: Protect your clarity and values from the surrounding darkness. Maintain your inner light without broadcasting it.
hexagrams.warning: Do not abandon your values because the environment does not reward them. The darkness is temporary.

hexagrams.name: Jia Ren
hexagrams.image: Wind comes forth from fire. The Family.
hexagrams.judgment: The Family. The perseverance of the woman furthers.
hexagrams.meaning: The Family hexagram speaks of the fundamental unit of human order — the relationships closest to you that shape everything else in your life. Wind fanning fire: the quality of the relationships in your inner circle either fans your potential or diminishes it. This hexagram calls attention to the health and alignment of your most intimate relationships — family, close friends, the people you live and work with daily. When these relationships are well-ordered, honest, and mutually supportive, everything flows outward from that stable centre.
hexagrams.guidance: Tend to your closest relationships. They are the foundation from which everything else in your life grows.
hexagrams.warning: Dysfunction in close relationships creates a centre of disorder that spreads outward. Address what needs addressing at home first.

hexagrams.name: Kui
hexagrams.image: Above, fire; below, the lake. Opposition.
hexagrams.judgment: Opposition. In small matters, good fortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Opposition speaks of two forces moving in opposite directions — fire rises, water descends — and the creative tension this polarity creates. In human relationships, opposition does not always mean conflict. Sometimes the greatest creativity comes from the meeting of opposites. This hexagram advises working with rather than against the differences you encounter. The opposition you face may be exactly what is needed to refine and sharpen what you are creating. Small steps are favoured now; grand unified moves are not. Find the common ground within the opposition.
hexagrams.guidance: Look for the common ground within the opposition. The tension you are experiencing may be generative rather than destructive.
hexagrams.warning: Do not try to eliminate all opposition or difference. Some tensions are creative rather than destructive.

hexagrams.name: Jian
hexagrams.image: Water on the mountain. Obstruction.
hexagrams.judgment: Obstruction. The southwest furthers. The northeast does not further. It furthers to see the great man. Perseverance brings good fortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Obstruction identifies a genuine block on your current path — not a sign that your goal is wrong, but a clear signal that the current approach is not working. Water cannot flow up a mountain. It must find another way. This hexagram counsels seeking the counsel of someone wiser or more experienced, and being willing to go around the obstruction rather than continuing to push against it directly. The goal remains valid. The path needs re-examination.
hexagrams.guidance: Seek the counsel of someone you respect greatly. A different perspective on this obstruction is exactly what is needed.
hexagrams.warning: Continuing to push against an immovable obstruction with the same approach is the definition of futility.

hexagrams.name: Xie
hexagrams.image: Thunder and rain set in. Deliverance.
hexagrams.judgment: Deliverance. The southwest furthers. If there is no longer anything where one has to go, return brings good fortune. If there is still something where one has to go, hastening brings good fortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Deliverance is the release of tension — thunder and rain after a long dry spell. The obstruction has been removed. What was stuck is now moving again. This hexagram often appears at the moment just after a difficult period breaks — when the difficulty is resolved and the energy is released. The advice is immediate: act swiftly to complete what needs completing and then stop. Do not prolong the action past its natural conclusion. Deliverance is a gift — receive it cleanly, act on it decisively, and let it complete.
hexagrams.guidance: The obstacle has been or is being removed. Act swiftly to complete what needs completing while the energy is moving.
hexagrams.warning: Do not drag out the resolution. Complete it cleanly and move on.

hexagrams.name: Sun
hexagrams.image: At the foot of the mountain, the lake. Decrease.
hexagrams.judgment: Decrease combined with sincerity brings about supreme good fortune without blame. One may be persevering in this.
hexagrams.meaning: Decrease speaks of a time when reduction is not loss but purification. The mountain and the lake are in balance: what is taken from below enriches what is above. Decreasing what is unnecessary, excessive, or harmful creates space for what is essential to flourish. This hexagram often appears when simplification, letting go, or a reduction of commitments and possessions would bring greater richness rather than poverty. What are you holding onto that is actually weighing you down?
hexagrams.guidance: Simplify. Let go of what is unnecessary. The reduction will feel like loss briefly and then like liberation.
hexagrams.warning: Not all decrease is purification. Discern carefully between what is truly excess and what is genuinely needed.

hexagrams.name: Yi
hexagrams.image: Wind and thunder. Increase.
hexagrams.judgment: Increase. It furthers one to undertake something. It furthers one to cross the great water.
hexagrams.meaning: Increase is the hexagram of genuine expansion and growth — a period when the conditions are aligned for real, lasting development. Wind and thunder working together amplify each other's effects. This is a time for bold, purposeful action. What you invest in now — in relationships, skills, projects, or your own development — will return manifold. Act with generosity. Expansive action taken with good intent during a period of Increase creates conditions for extraordinary results.
hexagrams.guidance: Move boldly. Invest generously. This is a period when what you put out comes back multiplied.
hexagrams.warning: Act with genuine good intent — increase gained through selfishness or exploitation does not last and creates future consequences.

hexagrams.name: Guai
hexagrams.image: The lake has risen up to heaven. Breakthrough.
hexagrams.judgment: Breakthrough. One must resolutely make the matter known at the court of the king.
hexagrams.meaning: Breakthrough is the hexagram of decisive, public resolution — the moment when something that has been building must be brought to a head. The lake has risen all the way to heaven — the pressure has reached its maximum and must be released. This hexagram often appears when something that has been known privately must now be stated clearly and publicly — a truth that needs speaking, a decision that needs announcing, a wrong that needs naming. The resolution must be approached with sincerity, clarity, and without aggression.
hexagrams.guidance: State clearly and publicly what needs to be said. The time for private hesitation has passed.
hexagrams.warning: Announce the breakthrough without hostility or self-righteousness. Truth stated aggressively loses its power.

hexagrams.name: Gou
hexagrams.image: Under heaven, wind. Coming to Meet.
hexagrams.judgment: Coming to Meet. The maiden is powerful. One should not marry such a maiden.
hexagrams.meaning: Coming to Meet speaks of an unexpected encounter — often a sudden appearance of something that seems attractive but carries hidden complications. A single feminine line at the bottom of six masculine lines: one small element that can gradually and subtly undermine the whole. This hexagram calls for careful discernment when something new appears and seems particularly appealing. Examine what is being presented with care. What is attractive on the surface may carry something problematic beneath it. Do not be swept away by initial impressions.
hexagrams.guidance: Examine new opportunities, people, or ideas carefully before committing to them. First impressions require testing.
hexagrams.warning: What appears most appealing right now may be exactly what requires most careful scrutiny.

hexagrams.name: Cui
hexagrams.image: Over the earth, the lake. Gathering Together.
hexagrams.judgment: Gathering Together. Success. The king approaches his temple. It furthers to see the great man.
hexagrams.meaning: Gathering Together speaks of the power of people assembling around a shared purpose or value — a congregation, a community, a movement. The lake gathers over the earth: many separate streams combining into something greater. This hexagram appears when the conditions are right for bringing people together — for collective action, shared celebration, or community building. The key is that the gathering must be centred on something genuine and meaningful, not mere convenience or entertainment. What centre would people genuinely gather around in your life right now?
hexagrams.guidance: Gather the right people around a genuine shared purpose. The time is right for collective action.
hexagrams.warning: Gatherings without a genuine centre tend to fragment. Ensure that what brings people together is real and lasting.

hexagrams.name: Sheng
hexagrams.image: Within the earth, wood grows upward. Pushing Upward.
hexagrams.judgment: Pushing Upward has supreme success. One must see the great man. Fear not. Departure to the south brings good fortune.
hexagrams.meaning: Pushing Upward is the hexagram of steady, organic growth upward — like a tree growing through the soil toward light. This is not a dramatic leap but a consistent, patient ascent. The tree does not force its way but pushes steadily and naturally. This hexagram appears during periods of gradual but real advancement — when your consistent effort is producing real growth that may not be immediately visible but is absolutely happening. The encouragement is to continue, to seek mentorship or guidance from someone who has walked this path before, and to trust the growth.
hexagrams.guidance: Continue your steady upward effort. Seek guidance from someone further along your path.
hexagrams.warning: Do not try to accelerate the ascent beyond its natural pace. Forced growth is weak growth.

hexagrams.name: Kun
hexagrams.image: There is no water in the lake. Exhaustion.
hexagrams.judgment: Exhaustion. Success. Perseverance. The great man brings about good fortune. No blame. When one has something to say, it is not believed.
hexagrams.meaning: Exhaustion describes a state of genuine depletion — the lake has no water, the resources are spent. This hexagram is unflinching in naming the difficulty: this is a hard time. What makes the great person visible in Exhaustion is not the absence of difficulty but the response to it. The great person does not collapse, does not abandon their values, and does not allow the difficulty to define them permanently. Words of complaint or explanation are not believed now — only consistent conduct over time will restore trust and credibility.
hexagrams.guidance: Maintain your integrity and continue your essential work through this difficult period. Do not explain yourself — demonstrate through action.
hexagrams.warning: Do not make important decisions while exhausted. Preserve your essential energy for what is most important.

hexagrams.name: Jing
hexagrams.image: Water over wood. The Well.
hexagrams.judgment: The Well. The town may be changed, but the well cannot be changed. It neither decreases nor increases.
hexagrams.meaning: The Well is one of the most profound hexagrams in the I Ching. The well is the unchanging source that nourishes the constantly changing village above it. Towns are built and fall; civilisations rise and collapse; but the well endures — constant, deep, accessible to all who know how to draw from it. This hexagram asks about your well — the deep source of your wisdom, creativity, and nourishment. Have you been tending it? Are you drawing from it properly? Is it accessible to others? A neglected well, or one with a broken bucket, fails the people who need it.
hexagrams.guidance: Tend to your deep source — whatever nourishes your wisdom and creativity. Keep it clear and accessible.
hexagrams.warning: A broken rope or a broken bucket makes the well useless even if the water is pure. Tend to the means of access as carefully as the source itself.

hexagrams.name: Ge
hexagrams.image: Fire in the lake. Revolution.
hexagrams.judgment: Revolution. On your own day you are believed. Supreme success, furthering through perseverance. Remorse disappears.
hexagrams.meaning: Revolution is the hexagram of fundamental, necessary change — the kind that transforms the underlying structure, not just the surface. Fire in the lake — two incompatible forces that cannot coexist indefinitely. Something must give. This hexagram appears when the time for incremental adjustment has passed and genuine transformation is both necessary and possible. Revolutions succeed when they arise from genuine necessity, are well-timed, and are carried out with clear purpose. Premature or unnecessary revolution destroys rather than transforms.
hexagrams.guidance: The time for fundamental change is now. Act with clarity, genuine necessity, and strong conviction.
hexagrams.warning: Do not mistake a preference for change with genuine necessity for revolution. Test your conviction carefully before acting.

hexagrams.name: Ding
hexagrams.image: Fire over wood. The Cauldron.
hexagrams.judgment: The Cauldron. Supreme good fortune. Success.
hexagrams.meaning: The Cauldron is the ritual vessel that transforms raw ingredients into nourishment fit for offering to the divine — and to those who depend on you. This is a hexagram of supreme transformation and the responsibility of nourishing others with the best of what you have and who you are. Whatever creative, spiritual, or practical work you are engaged in, this hexagram calls you to do it at the highest possible standard. The cauldron transforms — what enters as raw material exits as refined nourishment. Be worthy of this transformative function.
hexagrams.guidance: Bring your very best to what you are creating or offering. This is a time for your highest quality work.
hexagrams.warning: Offering what is impure, careless, or second-rate from the cauldron is a serious failure of responsibility.

hexagrams.name: Zhen
hexagrams.image: Thunder repeated. The Arousing.
hexagrams.judgment: The Arousing. Success. Shock comes — oh, oh! Laughing words — ha, ha! The shock terrifies for a hundred miles, and he does not let fall the sacrificial spoon and chalice.
hexagrams.meaning: The Arousing speaks of shock — sudden, startling, potentially frightening change that arrives like thunder. The person who responds rightly to shock is not paralysed by it but uses it to return to what is essential. The sacrificial spoon and chalice are not dropped: in the midst of chaos, the person of inner strength maintains what is most sacred to them. This hexagram appears when unexpected events, sudden reversals, or shocking news require you to draw on your deepest stability. The shock is real. It is also an awakening.
hexagrams.guidance: In the face of shock, return immediately to what is most essential and stable within you. Do not let the external upheaval displace your inner centre.
hexagrams.warning: Do not allow shock to drive hasty, reactive decisions. Stabilise first, then act.

hexagrams.name: Gen
hexagrams.image: Mountains standing close together. Keeping Still.
hexagrams.judgment: Keeping Still. Keeping his back still so that he no longer feels his body. He goes into his courtyard and does not see his people. No blame.
hexagrams.meaning: Keeping Still is the hexagram of profound, intentional stillness — the mountain that does not move. This is not the stillness of inertia or avoidance but the active, disciplined stillness of meditation, deep rest, and genuine non-action. The mind is stilled. The body is at rest. The moment is simply allowed to be. This hexagram appears when the most important thing you can do is genuinely stop — stop doing, stop planning, stop worrying — and allow stillness to do its work. In stillness, what is hidden becomes visible. What is unclear becomes clear.
hexagrams.guidance: Stop. Genuinely. Allow yourself a period of complete stillness. What becomes clear in the silence?
hexagrams.warning: Keeping still through discipline is different from paralysis through fear. Know which one you are experiencing.

hexagrams.name: Jian
hexagrams.image: On the mountain, a tree. Development.
hexagrams.judgment: Development. The maiden is given in marriage. Good fortune. Perseverance furthers.
hexagrams.meaning: Development is the hexagram of gradual, organic progress — like a tree growing on a mountain, slowly putting down roots and then reaching steadily upward year after year. The image of a marriage contract speaks of commitment to a long-term process: you cannot force a tree to grow faster by pulling it. You tend it, give it what it needs, and trust the natural pace of its unfolding. This hexagram is a clear instruction to resist the pressure of urgency and honour the natural timeline of what you are building.
hexagrams.guidance: Commit to the gradual process. Trust the pace that the situation naturally requires.
hexagrams.warning: Forcing development beyond its natural pace creates a tree with shallow roots that falls in the first wind.

hexagrams.name: Gui Mei
hexagrams.image: Thunder over the lake. The Marrying Maiden.
hexagrams.judgment: The Marrying Maiden. Undertakings bring misfortune. Nothing that would further.
hexagrams.meaning: The Marrying Maiden speaks of entering a relationship or situation from a position of necessity rather than genuine free choice — being given rather than choosing. This hexagram does not condemn the situation but it warns clearly: undertakings begun from this position carry the seeds of their own difficulty. When we enter arrangements out of need, desperation, or external pressure rather than genuine inner alignment, we diminish ourselves and compromise the arrangement. This hexagram calls for an honest examination of what you are agreeing to and why.
hexagrams.guidance: Examine carefully your motivations for what you are about to enter. Are you choosing freely or being driven by necessity?
hexagrams.warning: Agreements entered from a position of desperation rarely serve either party well. Strengthen your position before committing.

hexagrams.name: Feng
hexagrams.image: Thunder and lightning come together. Abundance.
hexagrams.judgment: Abundance has success. The king attains abundance. Be not sad. Be like the sun at midday.
hexagrams.meaning: Abundance is the hexagram of the fullest moment — the sun at its highest, the midday of a cycle. Everything is at its peak. This is a moment of genuine abundance, power, and fullness. The instruction is not merely to enjoy it but to be it fully — to inhabit this moment of fullness completely rather than already looking ahead to when it will diminish. The sun at midday does not mourn that evening is coming. It blazes. This hexagram calls you to inhabit your current fullness without reservation.
hexagrams.guidance: Be fully present in this abundant moment. Do not diminish it by anticipating its end.
hexagrams.warning: Abundance at its peak begins its descent. Use this fullness wisely — not to hoard, but to act, create, and share.

hexagrams.name: Lü
hexagrams.image: Fire on the mountain. The Wanderer.
hexagrams.judgment: The Wanderer. Success through smallness. Perseverance brings good fortune to the wanderer.
hexagrams.meaning: The Wanderer is in a foreign place — without the usual support structures, without established relationships, without a permanent base. This hexagram speaks to the condition of being in transition, between worlds, or genuinely uncertain of your permanent home. The fire on the mountain — burning brightly but not rooted — is the wanderer's image. The advice is to make the best of each stopping place without overstaying, to maintain impeccable behaviour because you have no established reputation to draw on, and to remain adaptable.
hexagrams.guidance: Maintain excellent conduct in all interactions. Your reputation in each new situation is built entirely from scratch.
hexagrams.warning: Do not attempt to establish permanent structures or make major commitments during a time of wandering. This is not the time.

hexagrams.name: Xun
hexagrams.image: Winds following one upon the other. The Gentle.
hexagrams.judgment: The Gentle. Success through small things. It furthers to have somewhere to go. It furthers to see the great man.
hexagrams.meaning: The Gentle is the wind that penetrates everywhere not through force but through consistent, pervasive, patient movement. It finds every crack, fills every space, and over time shapes even solid rock. This is the power of consistent, gentle influence — far more penetrating than dramatic force. This hexagram calls for patient, consistent action rather than dramatic gestures. Keep going. Keep moving in the right direction. The small, steady effort is accomplishing more than you can see.
hexagrams.guidance: Continue your gentle, consistent efforts. They are penetrating further than dramatic action would.
hexagrams.warning: The gentle wind that blows from too many directions at once loses its penetrating power. Focus your gentle effort.

hexagrams.name: Dui
hexagrams.image: Lakes resting one on the other. The Joyous.
hexagrams.judgment: The Joyous. Success. Perseverance is favourable.
hexagrams.meaning: The Joyous is the hexagram of genuine delight — not performed happiness but the authentic joy that arises from right relationships and right action. Two lakes resting on each other, each refreshing the other. Joy in the I Ching is not frivolous; it is the natural expression of alignment. When you are in the right place, with the right people, doing the right thing, joy arises naturally. This hexagram is an invitation to examine where genuine joy is present in your life and to cultivate more of those conditions.
hexagrams.guidance: Notice what brings you genuine joy and move toward more of those conditions. Joy is an important signal of alignment.
hexagrams.warning: Flattery and empty entertainment feel like joy but are not. Distinguish between genuine delight and hollow pleasure.

hexagrams.name: Huan
hexagrams.image: The wind drives over the water. Dispersion.
hexagrams.judgment: Dispersion. Success. The king approaches his temple. It furthers to cross the great water. Perseverance furthers.
hexagrams.meaning: Dispersion speaks of the dissolution of rigidity — the wind scattering what had become stuck or frozen. Ice melts. Separation dissolves. What had hardened and blocked the flow of energy between people or within a situation is breaking up. This hexagram often appears when alienation, selfishness, or hardness of heart need to be dissolved. The antidote to dispersion is gathering around a common centre — a shared value, a higher purpose, a sacred space that draws people together beyond their separateness.
hexagrams.guidance: Identify what has become rigid or frozen in your relationships or work. The energy is available now to dissolve it.
hexagrams.warning: Dispersion without a gathering centre creates chaos rather than renewal. Ensure there is something genuine to gather around.

hexagrams.name: Jie
hexagrams.image: Water over lake. Limitation.
hexagrams.judgment: Limitation. Success. Galling limitation must not be persevered in.
hexagrams.meaning: Limitation speaks of the importance of structure, boundaries, and appropriate regulation — not as restrictions on freedom but as the conditions that make meaningful action possible. A river without banks is a swamp. The limitation gives the water its direction and force. This hexagram calls for the establishment of clear, reasonable, meaningful limits — on your time, your commitments, your spending, your relationships. Not punitive limits but wise ones that channel energy productively.
hexagrams.guidance: Establish clear, wise limits that channel your energy effectively. What boundaries are you missing?
hexagrams.warning: Limits that are too severe or joyless become oppressive and generate resistance. Keep limits reasonable and purposeful.

hexagrams.name: Zhong Fu
hexagrams.image: Wind over lake. Inner Truth.
hexagrams.judgment: Inner Truth. Pigs and fishes. Good fortune. It furthers to cross the great water. Perseverance furthers.
hexagrams.meaning: Inner Truth is one of the most powerful hexagrams in the I Ching — the quality of genuine sincerity that penetrates even the most resistant hearts and situations. Wind moves over the lake and stirs it to its depths: this is the power of truth that comes from the very centre of a person. Even pigs and fish — the least responsive of creatures — are influenced by genuine inner truth. This hexagram calls you to examine the depth and genuineness of your own sincerity. Are you speaking and acting from your deepest truth?
hexagrams.guidance: Speak and act from your deepest truth. Genuine sincerity has an influence that strategy and cleverness cannot achieve.
hexagrams.warning: Sincerity that is only on the surface — that adjusts itself to what others want to hear — is not inner truth. It is manipulation dressed as openness.

hexagrams.name: Xiao Guo
hexagrams.image: Thunder on the mountain. Small Preponderance.
hexagrams.judgment: Small Preponderance. Success. Perseverance furthers. Small things may be done; great things should not be done.
hexagrams.meaning: Small Preponderance is the hexagram of the small surpassing the large — a time when modest, careful, small-scale action is what is called for. The thunder on the mountain: powerful but contained. This hexagram appears when the temptation is to take large, dramatic action but the actual situation calls for careful, modest, incremental steps. Great things should not be attempted now. Small things, done carefully, will bring success. This is not a limitation — it is wisdom about what this particular moment can hold.
hexagrams.guidance: Focus on small, careful steps. This is not the time for large ambitions — it is the time for precise, modest action.
hexagrams.warning: Attempting large actions when the situation calls for small ones creates excess that destabilises rather than advances.

hexagrams.name: Ji Ji
hexagrams.image: Water over fire. After Completion.
hexagrams.judgment: After Completion. Success in small matters. Perseverance furthers. At the beginning good fortune, at the end disorder.
hexagrams.meaning: After Completion is the hexagram of a cycle that has reached its conclusion — everything in its right place, order achieved, the task done. Water above fire: each element in its correct position, working in harmony. This is the most ordered hexagram in the I Ching, and it carries a subtle warning: completion is not permanence. The very perfection of this arrangement contains the seeds of its own disruption. After completion comes new disorder, new challenge, new beginning. The wise person does not rest on completion but uses it as a stable foundation for what comes next.
hexagrams.guidance: Acknowledge and appreciate this completion. Then immediately begin tending to what comes next.
hexagrams.warning: Rest too long in the completed state and disorder begins to return. Completion is a momentary achievement, not a permanent condition.

hexagrams.name: Wei Ji
hexagrams.image: Fire over water. Before Completion.
hexagrams.judgment: Before Completion. Success. But if the little fox, after nearly completing the crossing, gets his tail in the water, there is nothing that would further.
hexagrams.meaning: Before Completion ends the I Ching — not with resolution but with the threshold. Fire above water: each element out of its ideal position, the situation not yet resolved, the crossing not yet complete. The little fox almost reaches the other bank — and then gets his tail wet. The final error. Before Completion is a reminder that the most critical moment is often the last one — when fatigue sets in, when confidence makes one careless, when the end seems so close that vigilance relaxes. The I Ching ends here because completion is always provisional. Every ending is a new before-completion.
hexagrams.guidance: Maintain full vigilance and care right up to the very end of what you are doing. The final stretch is where things go wrong.
hexagrams.warning: Do not relax your care because the end seems near. The fox who gets his tail wet at the final moment undoes the entire crossing.
